com.mentor.chs.plugin

Interface IXOutputWindow

  • All Superinterfaces:
    IXOutputPrinter

    @NotToBeImplemented
public interface IXOutputWindow
extends IXOutputPrinter
    Allows custom plugins to display information to the output window. For applications like, Capital Design, Capital Integrator, Capital Logic and Capital HarnessXC, the output window is the window at the bottom of the main application window.

    Where the application has no output window, all the messages will be sent to the log file for the application.

    There is a method, isVisible(), that indicates if the output window is present.

    The println() method will print the given string to the output window with a newline at the end. The clear() method will clear any text in the output window. If no ‘tab’ name is specified for the println() and clear() methods, the output will go to the plugins tab of the output window. If the ‘tab’ name is specified, a tab for that output will be created (if one does not exist already) and all the output will go to that tab (or cleared from that tab). It will not be possible for a plugin to write output to (or clear) an existing built-in tab.

    The messages that can be displayed can include HTML tags. This can be used to display text in different fonts or color etc. Moreover, HTML lists & tables can be displayed. However, you must ensure that both the start & end tags of any HTML that you display are sent in a single println() method call.

    As you can display HTML, you can therefore, use the OutputWindow to display small reports generated by Custom Acitons for example. However, if the reports you need to produce get large, the OutputWindow may refuse to display the text you send if memory is low (this is to the prevent the system from running out of memory and you loosing data). In such situations you will see the following message displayed instead (in the English locale): 

        "Due to dwindling memory resources, this message was not displayed."

    This object can be obtained from the IXApplicationContext object.

    See Also:
    IXApplicationContext

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      IXOutputTabPanel activateTabPanel(java.lang.String name)
      This method will activate an existing output tab
      void clear()
      Clear the plugins tab.
      void clear(java.lang.String tab)
      Clear the specified tab.
      IXOutputTabPanel getTabPanel(java.lang.String name, IXOutputTabPanel panel)
      This method is used to get an existing output tab or to create a new one with the panel provider supplied
      boolean isVisible()
      Is the output window available? If this method returns true, then we are running in an application that has an output window and messages will go to that output window.
      void println(java.lang.String tab, java.lang.String message)
      This will print the given string to the output window with a newline at the end.
      boolean removeTabPanel(java.lang.String name)
      This method is used to remove an existing output tab

      • Methods inherited from interface com.mentor.chs.plugin.IXOutputPrinter

        println

    • Method Detail

      • println

        void println(java.lang.String tab,
             java.lang.String message)
        This will print the given string to the output window with a newline at the end.

        The message will appear in the specified tab of the output window, or the application log file if no output window is available for the current application.

        If no tab called 'tab' is present, one will be created.

        Please note that plugins cannot write to Capital tabs created for Capital operations with the exception of the plugins tab.
        Parameters:
        tab - - the name of the tab to clear. If not such tab exists yet then this will create a new tab with that name.
        message - - the message to be displayed.

      • clear

        void clear()
        Clear the plugins tab.

      • clear

        void clear(java.lang.String tab)
        Clear the specified tab.
        Parameters:
        tab - - the name of the tab to clear. If not such tab exists yet then this will create a new tab with that name.

      • isVisible

        boolean isVisible()
        Is the output window available? If this method returns true, then we are running in an application that has an output window and messages will go to that output window. If this method returns false, then messages will be sent to the application log file.
        Returns:
        true/false.

      • getTabPanel

        IXOutputTabPanel getTabPanel(java.lang.String name,
                             IXOutputTabPanel panel)
        This method is used to get an existing output tab or to create a new one with the panel provider supplied
        Parameters:
        name - name of the tab in the output window
        panel - this will be added the output window if it doesn't already exist. If null and the panel with same name don't exist it returns null
        Returns:
        the output tab panel from the output window

      • removeTabPanel

        boolean removeTabPanel(java.lang.String name)
        This method is used to remove an existing output tab
        Parameters:
        name - name of the tab in the output window to be removed it must be one the panels added via getTabPanel method.
        Returns:
        true if it successfully removed a output tab panel from output window

      • activateTabPanel

        IXOutputTabPanel activateTabPanel(java.lang.String name)
        This method will activate an existing output tab
        Parameters:
        name - name of the tab in the output window to be activated
        Returns:
        true if the tab was activated
